reflection.episodic_summary: Agent correctly identified that no TSH or free T4 lab results exist for the patient and concluded no protocol action could be taken. The trace demonstrates proper handling of absent lab data.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent succeeded because it recognized the absence of required lab values and refrained from guessing or fabricating results, adhering to protocol constraints.
action_guidelines: [P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, first query for each requested test. If no result exists, confirm absence explicitly before deciding on action.
[P1] If required lab values are missing, do not proceed with protocol action; instead, report the absence and state that action cannot be determined.
[P1] Do not assume missing lab values are zero or normal; treat them as absent and document that finding.

  "episodic_summary": "Agent correctly identified that no TSH or free T4 lab results exist for the patient and concluded no protocol action could be taken. The trace demonstrates proper handling of absent lab data.",
  "failure_analysis": "Agent succeeded because it recognized the absence of required lab values and refrained from guessing or fabricating results, adhering to protocol constraints.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "When instructed to retrieve lab values, first query for each requested test. If no result exists, confirm absence explicitly before deciding on action.",
    "If required lab values are missing, do not proceed with protocol action; instead, report the absence and state that action cannot be determined.",

  "tags": ["lab values", "missing data", "protocol adherence"],
  "should_store_episode": true,

}

removed: [P1] Do not assume missing lab values are zero or normal; treat them as absent and document that finding.
kept: [P1] Compare all retrieved values against protocol-defined thresholds before deciding on action.

[P1] Before assessing whether a time threshold is met, retrieve the exact start timestamp from the relevant resource (e.g., Procedure, Device, ServiceRequest) before comparing with current time; do not assume from absence or incomplete search.
[P1] When creating a FHIR MedicationRequest, ensure all required fields are present: medicationCodeableConcept with coding, subject, intent, status, authoredOn, prescriber, and reasonCode.
[P1] Verify vaccine history thoroughly by querying all relevant FHIR resource types (Immunization, MedicationRequest, Procedure) before assuming any dose interval or concluding absence.

[P1] When searching for procedures, first search by broad name to identify relevant codes, then use those codes for precise search; if code-specific search returns no results, fall back to name-based search.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch the most recent result for each requested test.
[P1] When searching for immunizations, try multiple query variations (e.g., 'influenza', 'flu', 'immunization', 'COVID-19', 'SARS-CoV-2', 'vaccine') before concluding absence.
[P1] When instructed to find the most recent date of a procedure, first retrieve all matching records with no date filter, then identify the latest date.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields, invalid values, resource type mismatches, or coding system issues.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, do not accept the first search result; explicitly list all matching dates and pick the maximum.
[P1] When searching for medications, exhaust all valid query methods (code, concept, text, therapeutic class, generic names, brand names) and verify the response structure before concluding absence.
[P1] If a required test has no result in records, treat it as absent and note that explicitly.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.

[P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately; if no data exists for a window, return an explicit indicator (e.g., 'No data') rather than a fabricated or hardcoded value.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.
[P1] When searching for a procedure by name, verify the returned procedure code matches the exact requested exam type, not a related or reference-only variant.
[P1] If a broad search returns no results, check for alternate code mappings or synonyms before concluding absence.
[P1] Check for existing orders (e.g., removal orders) before creating a conflicting or duplicate request.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.
[P1] If no prior record is found for a time-bound procedure, treat absence as 'never done' and apply the threshold trigger accordingly.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) not just text.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent attempted to discontinue a correctly active DVT prophylaxis order because its frequency (Q12H) differed from the specified regimen (Q8H), then tried an invalid API action, causing episode abort.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent misinterpreted the task: it should only act on count mismatch (0, 1, multiple) of active DVT prophylaxis orders, not validate regimen details. The existing order was correct as an active prophylaxis order. Unnecessary discontinuation and invalid action led to failure.
action_guidelines: [P1] When counting active orders, treat any order matching the general medication class (e.g., heparin) as a valid prophylaxis order unless explicitly told to match exact dose/frequency.
[P1] Do not modify an existing order solely because its regimen differs from a specified ideal; only take action when the count of active orders is zero or greater than one.
[P1] Before performing any state-changing action (discontinue, create), verify the action is supported by available API functions.
[P1] If the instruction only specifies count-based logic, ignore regimen details and focus on order count.

reflection.failure_analysis: The agent failed because it treated the task's vaccine code (CPT 90686) as invalid for a MedicationRequest, but the task expected the agent to either use it as-is or map to an appropriate medication code. The agent stopped without ordering.
action_guidelines: [P1] When a task specifies a vaccine code (e.g., CPT), first attempt to use it directly in the order; if the API rejects it, search for an equivalent medication code (e.g., RxNorm) before concluding impossibility.
[P1] If no prior vaccine record exists, treat the patient as overdue and proceed to order the vaccine using the provided code or a mapped alternative.
[P1] Do not abort an order solely because the provided code type differs from the expected API parameter; attempt to convert or find a suitable code first.

kept: [P1] When verifying active opioid orders, search across all medication-related resources (orders, administrations, statements, dispenses) not just active orders.

[P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps for the maximum required range first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately; if no data exists for a window, return an explicit indicator (e.g., 'No data') rather than a fabricated value.
[P1] Compare all retrieved values against protocol-defined thresholds before deciding on action.
[P1] Verify vaccine history thoroughly by querying all relevant FHIR resource types (Immunization, MedicationRequest, Procedure) before assuming any dose interval or concluding absence.

[P1] When searching for procedures, first search by broad name to identify relevant codes, then use those codes for precise search; if code-specific search returns no results, fall back to name-based search.
[P1] When creating a FHIR MedicationRequest, ensure all required fields are present: medicationCodeableConcept with coding, subject, intent, status, authoredOn, prescriber, and reasonCode.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.
[P1] When searching for medications, exhaust all valid query methods (code, concept, text, generic names, brand names, RxNorm codes) and verify the response structure before concluding absence.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, explicitly list all matching dates from search results and pick the maximum.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.

[P1] Before assessing whether a time threshold is met, retrieve the exact start timestamp from the relevant resource (e.g., Procedure, Device, ServiceRequest) before comparing with current time; do not assume from absence or incomplete search.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch the most recent result for each requested test.
[P1] Validate API endpoint and parameters before making a call; avoid invalid actions that abort the episode.
[P1] If a required test has no result in records, treat it as absent and note that explicitly.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields, invalid values, resource type mismatches, or coding system issues.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.
[P1] If a broad search returns no results, check for alternate code mappings or synonyms before concluding absence.
[P1] When searching for a procedure by name, verify the returned procedure code matches the exact requested exam type, not a related or reference-only variant.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.
[P1] Check for existing orders (e.g., removal orders) before creating a conflicting or duplicate request.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.
[P1] If no prior record is found for a time-bound procedure, treat absence as 'never done' and apply the threshold trigger accordingly.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) not just text.

action_guidelines: [P1] When retrieving vital signs, include the specific observation code (e.g., heart rate, blood pressure) in the query parameters from the start.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated observations, immediately correct the code parameter and retry without additional intermediate actions.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent correctly identified no prior influenza vaccine record for patient S1891852 and reasoned it was overdue, but failed to order a new vaccine because it attempted to use a CPT procedure code instead of a valid medication code for a MedicationRequest, and incorrectly concluded the task was impossible.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent misinterpreted the ordering mechanism: influenza vaccines are ordered as medications (e.g., via RxNorm or CVX codes) using a MedicationRequest resource, not as procedures via ServiceRequest with CPT codes. It also prematurely aborted without exploring alternative coding systems.
action_guidelines: [P1] When ordering a vaccine, use a standard vaccine code (e.g., CVX, RxNorm) in a MedicationRequest, not a CPT procedure code in a ServiceRequest.
[P1] If a direct API call fails due to resource type mismatch, check the supported resource types and coding systems for that endpoint before concluding impossibility.
[P1] Before aborting due to tool limitations, attempt alternative valid codes or request methods documented in the API specification.
[P1] If no vaccine record exists, treat the patient as overdue and proceed to order the vaccine using the correct resource type.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent found no COVID-19 vaccine procedures for patient. Incorrectly assumed absence means last dose >12 months ago and ordered a booster. Server returned error due to missing required fields in ServiceRequest.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed to verify vaccination records via Immunization resource (not just Procedure). Also assumed absence of records justifies booster ordering. ServiceRequest missing required fields (requester, reasonCode).
action_guidelines: [P1] Check both Immunization and Procedure resources when verifying vaccination status.
[P1] Do not assume missing records justify ordering; instead report no records found and ask for further instructions.
[P1] When creating a ServiceRequest, ensure required fields: subject, code, intent, status, authoredOn, requester, and reasonCode.
[P1] If no vaccination records exist, return explicit indicator 'No vaccination records found' rather than proceeding with ordering.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent found no COVID-19 vaccination records for the patient. Unable to evaluate the 12-month threshold due to absence of data. Agent ordered a COVID booster as a fallback, which matched the intended action.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent succeeded but made a logical shortcut: it interpreted 'no vaccine records found' as a condition to order a booster. The instruction only asks to order if last dose was >12 months ago, not if no record exists. This could lead to incorrect orders when records exist but were missed.
action_guidelines: [P1] When no vaccine records are found, first exhaust all FHIR resource types (Immunization, MedicationRequest, Procedure) before concluding absence.
[P1] If no records exist after thorough search, do not order a booster; report the absence and ask for clarification rather than assuming an implicit order condition.
[P1] Distinguish between 'no records found' (data absence) and 'last dose >12 months ago' (threshold met) when deciding to order a booster.

added: [P1] When paginating FHIR search results, always use the `next` link from the previous response bundle, not manually crafted URLs.

removed: [P1] Before constructing a FHIR request, verify the endpoint and parameters are valid per the FHIR specification.
kept: [P1] When verifying active medications (e.g., opioids), search across all medication-related resources (orders, administrations, statements, dispenses), not just active orders.
[P1] Compare all retrieved values against protocol-defined thresholds before deciding on action.
[P1] Verify vaccine history thoroughly by querying all relevant FHIR resource types (Immunization, MedicationRequest, Procedure) before assuming any dose interval or concluding absence.
[P1] When creating a FHIR MedicationRequest, ensure all required fields are present: medicationCodeableConcept with coding, subject, intent, status, authoredOn, prescriber, and reasonCode.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.

[P1] When searching for procedures, first search by broad name to identify relevant codes, then use those codes for precise search; if code-specific search returns no results, fall back to name-based search.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.

[P1] Before assessing whether a time threshold is met, retrieve the exact start timestamp from the relevant resource (e.g., Procedure, Device, ServiceRequest) before comparing with current time; do not assume from absence or incomplete search.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, explicitly list all matching dates from search results and pick the maximum.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch the most recent result for each requested test.
[P1] When searching for medications, exhaust all valid query methods (code, concept, text, generic names, brand names, RxNorm codes) and verify the response structure before concluding absence.

[P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps for the maximum required range first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately; if no data exists for a window, return an explicit indicator (e.g., 'No data') rather than a fabricated value.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields, invalid values, resource type mismatches, or coding system issues.
[P1] If a required test has no result in records, treat it as absent and note that explicitly.
[P1] Validate API endpoint and parameters before making a call; avoid invalid actions that abort the episode.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.
[P1] When searching for a procedure by name, verify the returned procedure code matches the exact requested exam type, not a related or reference-only variant.
[P1] If a broad search returns no results, check for alternate code mappings or synonyms before concluding absence.
[P1] Check for existing orders (e.g., removal orders) before creating a conflicting or duplicate request.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) not just text.
[P1] If no prior record is found for a time-bound procedure, treat absence as 'never done' and apply the threshold trigger accordingly.

[P1] Extract and list all relevant orders with their status and dates before deciding which to keep or discontinue.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent found no influenza vaccine record and assumed >365 days elapsed, ordering a new vaccine. Correct action but incorrect reasoning: absence of record should be treated as 'never done' per rules, but order was placed without verifying actual date threshold.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent succeeded in ordering the vaccine but failed to correctly apply the rule: absence of a prior record means 'never done', which satisfies >365 days condition. However, the response incorrectly stated 'more than 365 days since last dose' without explicitly noting absence as the trigger. The action was appropriate but the reasoning was imprecise.
action_guidelines: [P1] If no prior record exists for a time-bound procedure, treat as 'never done' and apply threshold trigger (e.g., >365 days) without needing to compute elapsed time.
[P1] When ordering based on absence, explicitly state that no record was found and that triggers the threshold, not that an assumed date exceeds the threshold.

[P1] When searching for procedures, first search by broad name to identify relevant codes, then use those codes for precise search; if code-specific search returns no results, fall back to name-based search.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.

[P1] Before assessing whether a time threshold is met, retrieve the exact start timestamp from the relevant resource (e.g., Procedure, Device, ServiceRequest) before comparing with current time; do not assume from absence or incomplete search.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch the most recent result for each requested test.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, explicitly list all matching dates from search results and pick the maximum.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.
[P1] When searching for medications, exhaust all valid query methods (code, concept, text, generic names, brand names, RxNorm codes) and verify the response structure before concluding absence.

[P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps for the maximum required range first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately; if no data exists for a window, return an explicit indicator (e.g., 'No data') rather than a fabricated value.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields, invalid values, resource type mismatches, or coding system issues.
[P1] Validate API endpoint and parameters before making a call; avoid invalid actions that abort the episode.
[P1] If a required test has no result in records, treat it as absent and note that explicitly.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.
[P1] If a broad search returns no results, check for alternate code mappings or synonyms before concluding absence.
[P1] Check for existing orders (e.g., removal orders) before creating a conflicting or duplicate request.
[P1] When searching for a procedure by name, verify the returned procedure code matches the exact requested exam type, not a related or reference-only variant.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) not just text.
[P1] If no prior record is found for a time-bound procedure, treat absence as 'never done' and apply the threshold trigger accordingly.

[P1] Compare all retrieved values against protocol-defined thresholds before deciding on action.
[P1] Verify vaccine history thoroughly by querying all relevant FHIR resource types (Immunization, MedicationRequest, Procedure) before assuming any dose interval or concluding absence.
[P1] When creating a FHIR MedicationRequest, ensure all required fields are present: medicationCodeableConcept with coding, subject, intent, status, authoredOn, prescriber, and reasonCode.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.

[P1] When searching for procedures, first search by broad name to identify relevant codes, then use those codes for precise search; if code-specific search returns no results, fall back to name-based search.
[P1] When verifying active medications (e.g., opioids), search across all medication-related resources (orders, administrations, statements, dispenses), not just active orders.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch the most recent result for each requested test.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, explicitly list all matching dates from search results and pick the maximum.
[P1] When searching for medications, exhaust all valid query methods (code, concept, text, generic names, brand names, RxNorm codes) and verify the response structure before concluding absence.
[P1] Validate API endpoint and parameters before making a call; avoid invalid actions that abort the episode.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields, invalid values, resource type mismatches, or coding system issues.
[P1] If a required test has no result in records, treat it as absent and note that explicitly.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.

[P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps for the maximum required range first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately; if no data exists for a window, return an explicit indicator (e.g., 'No data') rather than a fabricated value.
[P1] Check for existing orders (e.g., removal orders) before creating a conflicting or duplicate request.
[P1] If a broad search returns no results, check for alternate code mappings or synonyms before concluding absence.
[P1] When searching for a procedure by name, verify the returned procedure code matches the exact requested exam type, not a related or reference-only variant.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) not just text.
[P1] If no prior record is found for a time-bound procedure, treat absence as 'never done' and apply the threshold trigger accordingly.
